title: MockChain Technical Specification
subtitle: A deterministic fixture document used to exercise the pipeline with zero API spend.

blocks.text: MockChain is a fixture project used to drive the full generation pipeline without any model calls. This document mirrors the shape of a real Tech Spec so the renderer, gate, and Context Store all behave as they would in production.

blocks.label: Key Takeaway
blocks.text: Every section here is deterministic, so mock runs are reproducible end to end.
Testing a multi-agent pipeline against a live model is slow and costs credits. Teams need a way to validate orchestration, the QA gate loop, and persistence without spending on inference.

items: Live runs cost money per stage
Non-determinism makes regressions hard to reproduce
Credentials are required just to smoke-test the flow

blocks.text: A fixture mode short-circuits each agent to return a canned result, so the orchestrator runs unchanged while the model is never called. The gate reads a configured verdict to make the loop deterministic.

blocks.label: Design
blocks.text: Fixtures are valid against the same parsers real outputs use, so a mock run renders a real PDF.
The Context Store groups every write under a single generation id and records items, runs, artifacts, and snapshots. Domain data rides in opaque metadata and tags.
A single persist endpoint accepts a batch of any subset of writes for one generation id and returns an acknowledgement. Failures are non-fatal so generation never blocks on durability.

items: POST /api/dcl/persist accepts items, run, artifact, snapshot
Best-effort writes: errors are logged and swallowed
Store selected by an environment flag

blocks.text: The orchestrator mints one generation id at the start of a run and threads it through every checkpoint. In mock mode the client returns fixtures directly and skips the job plumbing.

blocks.label: Note
blocks.text: The download button stays enabled in every deliverable state.
The service-role key never reaches the browser; all durable writes go through a server route. Mock mode needs no credentials at all, reducing the blast radius of local testing.

items: Read path deferred to a later phase
No third-party client library that opens sockets at import

blocks.text: The loop policy is a pure function with a full decision-table unit test. The store has a round-trip test, and the persist path is covered without a live backend.

blocks.label: Coverage
blocks.text: Deterministic verdicts let a single run prove the force-pass path terminates.
Phase 1 ships the gate, the write-only store, and mock mode. Phase 2 adds the cross-session read path without any schema change.
Mock runs cost nothing. Live runs bill per stage at each model's published rate; the gate defaults to the most capable judge and can be switched to a cheaper one.
